verbose_sections.rationale: Explanatory catalogue of supported dev types and when-to-script guidance. This is orientation prose for humans, not executable orchestrator rules. Dev types are a static enumeration that agents derive from context; the when-to-script heuristics belong in agents/dev.md, not the orchestrator command.
Full worked example that narrates the workflow steps already documented above. Redundant with the step-by-step workflow in Implementation. A 42-line example adds no executable rule content.

Contains 'Why this rule exists' incident narrative and two WHAT/HOW worked examples. The rule itself (9 lines) is substantive. The backstory (~20 lines) and examples (~25 lines) are explanatory fluff. The hook at pretool-orchestrator-prompt-purity.py is the enforcement mechanism, not this prose.

Duplicates quality standard summaries already defined in agents/dev.md and agents/qa.md. Belongs in /dev.md agent files, not the orchestrator command. Cross-references via 'see agents/dev.md' confirm it is redundant overview.

File-naming conventions and retention policy. The conventions are already encoded in every step's prompt template; the retention note (archive after 30 days via /clean) is documentation, not a rule the orchestrator executes.
Three-bullet summary of /clean behavior. Not executed by /dev; belongs in /clean documentation.
Six checkbox aspirations (100% clarity, root cause, etc.). These are orientation prose, not orchestrator execution rules. They duplicate acceptance criteria expressed in each step.

findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: remove Agent Development Use Cases (133 lines), Example End-to-End Workflow (42 lines), Quality Standards Enforcement (23 lines), JSON Storage Policy (19 lines), Integration with /clean (11 lines), Success Metrics (15 lines), and trim Orchestrator Prompt Purity to the rule block only (remove the 'Why this rule exists' backstory ~20 lines and the WHAT/HOW worked examples ~25 lines — keep the enforcement rule list only). Target reduction: ~268 lines (20% -> ~2%). The Orchestrator Prompt Purity rule itself is MANDATORY and must be retained. Reference: convert.md cleanup (commit 2d21631) reduced 113 lines (-22%) by removing similar verbose sections.

verbose_sections.rationale: Six anti-pattern blocks with BAD/GOOD pairs and step-by-step decimal numbering examples. The prohibition rules are already encoded in the Quality Checklist and Minimum-Diff Rule. The BAD/GOOD examples add explanatory context but not new executable constraints.

Full worked example (diff budget declaration, Read, Edit, verify steps, counter-example). The rules are already in the Minimum-Diff Rule and CRITICAL: Execution Discipline sections. Examples explain but do not add executable rules.
Five-sentence explanation of how dev-report is consumed by QA. This is orientation prose; QA reads dev-report directly — the integration is structural, not a rule agents need to execute.

findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: trim or remove Common Anti-Patterns to Avoid (63 lines — retain prohibitions as 1-line bullets, remove code blocks), trim Example Execution to 5-line summary (remove step-by-step walkthrough, keep diff_stats example only), remove Integration with QA Subagent (15 lines — self-evident from Output Format). Target reduction: ~115 lines (13% -> ~1%). Reference: convert.md cleanup (commit 2d21631) reduced 113 lines (-22%) by removing similar verbose sections.

verbose_sections.rationale: Many of the forbidden patterns are articulated as BAD/GOOD pairs with extended prose rationale. The actual prohibition list is actionable; the explanatory context (~30 lines of why/backstory prose) exceeds 'rules not stories' threshold.
Full worked example with step-by-step narrative. Rules are already in the Verification Process section. 72 lines of example content for an agent that should verify quickly is verbose.

45-line section explaining spec update mechanics that are owned by agents/dev.md and the orchestrator. QA's role is to report proposed updates via qa-report JSON, not to execute spec writes. The 'After Verification' subsection contains orientation prose that duplicates orchestrator-side instructions.

findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: trim Forbidden QA Patterns to prohibition list only (remove explanatory prose, ~30 lines), compress Example Execution to a 10-line skeleton (remove step-by-step narrative), trim Overnight Spec Integration to the qa.spec_section_updates JSON contract (remove prose rationale, ~30 lines). Target reduction: ~130 lines (9.9% -> ~3%). Reference: convert.md cleanup (commit 2d21631) reduced 113 lines (-22%) by removing similar verbose sections.
No verbose sections detected. File is concise and execution-focused. No action required.
No verbose sections detected. File contains operational workflow phases with precise procedural rules. No action required.

summary.estimated_reduction: commands/dev.md: ~18% reduction available; agents/dev.md: ~12% reduction available; agents/qa.md: ~7% reduction available
summary.notes: All three files with violations (commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md, agents/qa.md) contain pre-existing verbosity. None of the verbose sections were introduced in this diff (cycle 20260522-080646 added hook/test infrastructure files, not documentation prose). All findings tagged introduced_in_diff: false per AC-12.1 Mechanism (ii). These findings are advisory and non-blocking for CLOSE per the default-safe rule: absent a positive introduced_in_diff marker on a finding, close.md treats it as pre-existing. The changed .sh/.py files (hooks/tests/, hooks/lib/) are not command/agent documentation and are out of scope for verbosity inspection.
